conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility, can you tell me: do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you.

Next, I need to ask: Are you the person responsible for Alex? This means you're responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and they normally live with you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't actually live with me. They live with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is important for determining eligibility.

Can you tell me: Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep? (Currently, Child Benefit is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more question to complete the assessment:

Is there any particular reason why Alex lives with their other parent rather than with you? For example, is Alex in hospital, in care, or are there any other special circumstances?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's nothing like that. Alex just lives with their other parent - it's a normal living arrangement. Their mum is the one who looks after them day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you must be responsible for the child, which means:
- You must be responsible for their day-to-day care, AND
- The child must normally live with you

Since Alex lives with their other parent who provides the day-to-day care, you don't meet this requirement.

There is an exception where you can claim Child Benefit even if the child doesn't live with you - but only if you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child) towards the child's upkeep. Since you're not currently contributing financially, this exception doesn't apply to you either.

The person who would be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex is their other parent, as they are the one providing day-to-day care and Alex lives with them.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
circumstances: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old and therefore under 16, which meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. However, the claimant does not meet the key eligibility criteria. According to the rules, to claim Child Benefit you must be responsible for the child, which means being responsible for their day-to-day care and the child must normally live with you. Alex lives with their other parent who provides the day-to-day care. Additionally, when a child lives with someone else, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate towards the child's upkeep. The claimant has confirmed they do not contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. While Alex meets the age requirements, the claimant fails to meet the fundamental eligibility criteria as they do not have day-to-day responsibility for Alex (who lives with the other parent), and they do not contribute financially towards Alex's upkeep at a level that would allow them to claim when the child lives elsewhere.
